category_title: Adolescent with Down's syndrome exhibiting verbal outbursts to escape demands or gain tangibles
scenario_title: Access to preferred food items during lunch
minimal_context: Dennis is sitting in the cafeteria at the beginning of lunch while the teacher places his preferred food item in sight but out of reach.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: (Sits next to Dennis and places his preferred food item in sight but out of reach) You can have this in a minute.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: We just need to wait a little bit.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: (Sits next to Dennis and places his preferred food item in sight but out of reach) You can have this in a minute.

STUDENT: (Reaches toward the food item) No.

TUTOR: We just need to wait a little bit.

STUDENT: (Gestures at the food) Stop it.

TUTOR: Almost time.

STUDENT: That's enough! No!

context.social_setting: teacher sat next to the participant

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: teacher sat next to the participant and placed the preferred food item in sight but out of the participant’s reach. The teacher then told the participant, “You can have this in a minute.”
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: verbal outbursts such as “No” or “Stop it.”

behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: engaged in the highest levels of challenging behavior during the tangible test condition (35% of trials)
